ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'milk tea' class (FOODON_03301103). No specific entry exists for wildflower honey oolong tea latte; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers tea-based beverages broadly. No specific code exists for honey oolong milk tea; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 represents milk tea as a substance class. No specific code for honey oolong tea latte; closest_parent used.

preparation_method: served chilled or at room temperature

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium-sized cup (approximately 500 ml) as typically served at KOI Thé in Singapore, containing oolong tea brewed with fresh milk and wildflower honey.

nutrient_source.title: Milk Tea, ready-to-drink, estimated from component analysis: oolong tea, whole milk, and honey
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy) — component estimation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for wildflower honey oolong tea latte exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values estimated from weighted component analysis: approximately 60% brewed oolong tea (USDA FDC #171917, essentially water with trace nutrients), 35% whole milk (USDA FDC #746782), and 5% wildflower honey (USDA FDC #169640). Medium cup assumed at 500 ml. Per-100g values derived from this blend. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of milk and honey-sweetened beverages — analogous estimate based on honey (GI ~55) and whole milk (GI ~27) blend; oolong tea contributes negligible GI
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for wildflower honey oolong tea latte. GI estimated as a weighted blend: whole milk GI ~27 (University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008), wildflower honey GI ~55 (University of Sydney GI Database), and brewed oolong tea GI ~1 (effectively zero glycemic carbohydrates). Weighted by approximate carbohydrate contribution: milk ~40%, honey ~55%, tea ~5%. Blended GI estimated at ~35, categorised as low. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (35 × 36.0 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 12.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~90 mg per 500 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 18 mg sodium per 100 g (90 mg per medium cup), this beverage poses minimal sodium concern for individuals managing hypertension, though daily added-sugar intake from honey should still be monitored.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Free sugars from wildflower honey (~34 g per 500 ml serving) and moderate glycemic load (~12.6)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this beverage due to approximately 34 g of free sugars per medium cup derived from wildflower honey; the glycemic load of ~12.6 per serving is moderate and may require insulin or medication adjustment if consumed regularly.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~4 g per 500 ml serving) and beneficial polyphenols from oolong tea
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains modest saturated fat (~4 g per medium cup) from whole milk and provides oolong tea polyphenols that may support vascular health; however, regular consumption of honey-sweetened beverages should be limited to avoid excess caloric and sugar intake in cardiovascular disease management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wildflower Honey Oolong Tea Latte (Medium) is a Taiwanese-style milk tea beverage sold at KOI Thé outlets in Singapore, including the Funan location. It is prepared by brewing high-quality oolong tea leaves and blending the infusion with fresh whole milk and natural wildflower honey, resulting in a smooth, lightly floral, and subtly sweet drink with a creamy mouthfeel. The oolong tea base contributes a characteristic roasted-floral aroma and mild tannin character, while the wildflower honey adds a nuanced sweetness distinct from refined sugar. The fresh milk provides a creamy texture and contributes calcium, protein, and riboflavin. Per 100 g, the beverage contains approximately 46 kcal, 1.2 g protein, 1.3 g fat, and 7.2 g carbohydrate, predominantly from honey-derived sugars. A standard medium serving of 500 ml delivers approximately 230 kcal and 36 g of carbohydrate. The glycemic index is estimated at 35 (low), though the glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 12.6 due to the volume of honey used. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor intake due to free sugar content. The beverage is low in sodium and suitable for those managing hypertension. It is vegetarian, halal-friendly, and gluten-free, but contains dairy and is not suitable for lactose-intolerant individuals or vegans. This drink is popular in Singapore's bubble tea and specialty tea culture, representing the Taiwanese tea-shop tradition of premium milk teas with natural sweeteners.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for wildflower honey oolong tea latte exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from a weighted component analysis of brewed oolong tea, whole milk, and wildflower honey using USDA FoodData Central approved source data. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database reference values for honey and milk. Confidence is medium due to estimation methodology.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from component analysis (60% brewed oolong tea, 35% whole milk, 5% wildflower honey) using USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods data. Serving size assumed at 500 ml (medium cup) based on standard KOI Thé medium cup volume. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA whole milk and honey data; trace amounts below detection threshold. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a liquid beverage with no fibrous plant matter retained after brewing. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central for the component ingredients (oolong tea, whole milk, honey) used in this beverage blend. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central for the component ingredients (oolong tea, whole milk, honey) used in this beverage blend. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central for the component ingredients (oolong tea, whole milk, honey) used in this beverage blend. glycemic_index set to 35 (low category) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database values for honey (~55) and whole milk (~27), weighted by carbohydrate contribution. Glycemic load per 500 g serving calculated as (35 × 36.0) / 100 = 12.6. The food_name prefix 'M-' in the input record denotes medium cup size; canonical English name normalised to 'Wildflower Honey Oolong Tea Latte (Medium)'. This record originates from field survey data seed data for KOI Thé - Funan.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full record from component estimation using USDA FoodData Central. Added multilingual names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi as required for Singapore-served Taiwanese dish. Added ontology codes with closest_parent match type. Added GI analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database.

Iteration 2: Addressed G3 numeric hygiene by explicitly documenting the source (USDA FoodData Central) for all -1 sentinel values in the 'notes' field. Addressed Q8 aliases consistency failure by verifying Hindi alias character match. Verified all zero values are justified. Verified GI > 0. Verified ontology match_type values use only allowed strings.

Iteration 3: Addressed Q8 aliases_by_language entries missing from flat aliases. Root cause identified: 'Yě Huā Mì Xiān Nǎi Wūlóng Chá (Zhōng Bēi)' appeared in aliases_by_language.romanized but was absent from the flat aliases array. Added this entry to the flat aliases list. Performed full exhaustive cross-check of all 20 aliases_by_language string values against the flat aliases array to confirm complete consistency. All entries now verified verbatim-present in both structures.
